lesson.menu_desc: Reflect on your progress -- present perfect, strengths and goals -- 28 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 8 -- Block 1 Review -- reflect on your progress
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Rubens Tofolo | Aula 8 -- Block 1 Review

listenings.text: Hello again, and welcome to your first review session. Before we continue, let us look back at everything you have done in Block 1. In only seven lessons, you have reactivated your English and built a strong base. You have talked about your daily routine at the clinic, described the past, and shared your life experiences. You have learned to describe people and places, to make plans for a trip, and to order food politely at a restaurant. Your biggest strength is clear: you are not afraid to speak, and your confidence has grown every single week. Of course, you still make a few mistakes with verb tenses, but that is completely normal, and you always learn from them. My advice is simple. Keep your progress steady, review a little every day, and set one small goal for each lesson. You have come a long way, and the best part of the journey is still ahead of you.

When I started Block 1, I could only say a few words in English, and I felt nervous in every single class. Now, after seven lessons, I notice a real difference. I have improved a lot, and I feel more confident when I speak. My favorite lesson was the restaurant, because I learned to order food politely, and I use those phrases when I travel. My biggest goal is to speak with my international colleagues at a medical congress without fear. I know I still have some weaknesses, especially with the past tense, so I review my notes for ten minutes every morning. I also ask my teacher for honest feedback after each class, because good feedback helps me grow. My progress is slow and steady, but it is real. I am proud of how far I have come, and I am ready for the next block.

extra_audio.text: Here is how I review my progress in English. First, I look back at the lessons I have finished. Then I write down my strengths and my weaknesses. After that, I choose one small goal for the next class. Next, I ask my teacher for feedback and practice the difficult parts. Finally, I review a little every day to keep my progress steady.
